data.body:
[G4 child-consistency re-audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (908c link-consumer) vs whole roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-CLEAR consumer)]

7 cross-child interaction modes, high-confidence + benign-reading filter, verified against code:

1. Implicit shared state — `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are explicitly parent-owned (parent What/Scope); both children only READ. Reducer fold + serialization is parent in-scope work. No undeclared shared mutation. CLEAN.
2. Conflicting assumptions — gate semantics agree across parent + both children: peer ref present + locally absent ⇒ REMOVE/CLEAR iff ∈ managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, NO inbound-remove dual. 908c ("NO inbound-remove dual … consistent with parent") aligns with ba2c and parent. CLEAN.
3. Dependency gap — 908c depends on the parent primitive/gate; parent-child hierarchy (both children parent_id b4d4) supplies ordering; parent Scope tracks the two consumers. CLEAN.
4. Scope overlap — 908c = LINK path (`_diff_links`, acli `delete_relationship`, batch_dispatch link REMOVE, inbound link-ADD suppression); ba2c = PARENT-CLEAR path. Disjoint code paths. Root-cause cites verified: outbound `_diff_links` ADD-only ("No REMOVE mutations are emitted", outbound_differ.py:1020); shared gate is common CONSUMPTION not duplicated logic. CLEAN.
5. Ordering violation — independent consumers; no inter-child ordering dependency. CLEAN.
6. Consumer impact — both extend same-pass inbound suppression on DISJOINT keys (908c link-add echo vs ba2c parent/scalar adoption). No cross-interference. CLEAN.
7. Residual references — parent alias "safe-luge-nog" confirmed = b4d4. CLEAN.

NON-G4 note (within-child / G2-class, reaffirmed): Jira "Blocks" collapses both `blocks` and `depends_on` direction-agnostically in `_existing_jira_links` ((type_name,key), outbound_differ.py:995-998), while parent `managed_refs` keys on direction-distinguished `(kind,target)`. 908c's link-REMOVE gate lookup must recover direction from the Jira link's inward/outward side (as inbound already does, inbound_differ.py:492-502) before probing `(blocks,target)` vs `(depends_on,target)`. This is resolvable inside 908c's own implementation and does NOT contradict parent normalization or sibling ba2c (single-direction `(parent,target)`); a within-child underspecification, not a cross-child interaction defect.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).
